title: Human Approval Boundary Packet Briefing
summary: Notes about when AI-generated operational recommendations can act automatically versus require review. Briefing focus: Recommend which Briefing Genie actions can be automated and which should require human approval.

claims.text: Governance workshop: Participants approved automatic drafting, summarization, and artifact linking when outputs are clearly marked as generated.
Operations incident note: A prior automation posted a recommendation as if it were an approved decision, causing two teams to pause rollout work unnecessarily.
Product analytics: Users are most likely to accept generated briefings when the interface separates evidence, recommendation, and action controls.

openQuestions: Confirm whether legal review has newer follow-up data.
Confirm whether action taxonomy draft has newer follow-up data.
recommendation: Use this as a fast first pass, then inspect source tension and citation support in the lab before treating the recommendation as shippable.
